responseMessageContents.text: The localtunnel expired. I'll create a fresh, permanent-ish tunnel.

input.command:
cd /home/user/rork-app/expo/android/app/build/outputs/apk/release

# Restart server if needed
kill -0 $(cat /tmp/apk-server-pid.txt 2>/dev/null) 2>/dev/null || {
  fuser -k 8080/tcp 2>/dev/null
  sleep 1
  nohup python3 -m http.server 8080 --bind 0.0.0.0 > /tmp/apk-server.log 2>&1 &
  echo $! > /tmp/apk-server-pid.txt
  sleep 2
  echo "Server restarted"
}

# Kill any stale localtunnel processes
pkill -f localtunnel 2>/dev/null || true
sleep 1

# Start a new tunnel with persistent output
nohup bunx --yes localtunnel --port 8080 > /tmp/lt-output.txt 2>&1 &
LT_PID=$!
echo $LT_PID > /tmp/lt-pid.txt
sleep 12

echo "=== localtunnel output ==="
cat /tmp/lt-output.txt

echo ""
echo "=== Extracted URL ==="
grep -oP 'https://[a-z0-9-]+\.loca\.lt' /tmp/lt-output.txt | head -1

echo ""
echo "=== Test download via tunnel ==="
LT_URL=$(grep -oP 'https://[a-z0-9-]+\.loca\.lt' /tmp/lt-output.txt | head -1)
if [ -n "$LT_URL" ]; then
  curl -sI "$LT_URL/app-release.apk" | head -15
fi

responseMessageContents.text: I'll ignore the incorrect "new project" context — this is the existing IVX Holdings Expo project. The tunnel command got exit code 128 (likely killed by `pkill -f localtunnel` matching itself). Let me create a fresh tunnel correctly.

input.command:
cd /home/user/rork-app/expo/android/app/build/outputs/apk/release

# Verify server is running
kill -0 $(cat /tmp/apk-server-pid.txt 2>/dev/null) 2>/dev/null && echo "Server running: OK" || {
  fuser -k 8080/tcp 2>/dev/null
  sleep 1
  nohup python3 -m http.server 8080 --bind 0.0.0.0 > /tmp/apk-server.log 2>&1 &
  echo $! > /tmp/apk-server-pid.txt
  sleep 2
  kill -0 $(cat /tmp/apk-server-pid.txt) && echo "Server restarted: OK" || echo "Server restart failed"
}

# Clean up localtunnel PID
cat /tmp/lt-pid.txt 2>/dev/null | xargs -r kill 2>/dev/null || true
sleep 2

# Start new tunnel without pkill killing it
bunx --yes localtunnel --port 8080 > /tmp/lt-output.txt 2>&1 &
LT_PID=$!
echo $LT_PID > /tmp/lt-pid.txt
sleep 15

echo "=== Tunnel output ==="
cat /tmp/lt-output.txt

echo ""
LT_URL=$(grep -oP 'https://[a-z0-9-]+\.loca\.lt' /tmp/lt-output.txt | head -1)
if [ -n "$LT_URL" ]; then
  echo "New download link: $LT_URL/app-release.apk"
  echo ""
  echo "=== Testing tunnel ==="
  curl -sI "$LT_URL/app-release.apk" | head -10
else
  echo "No tunnel URL found. Trying alternative..."
  cat /tmp/lt-output.txt
fi

output.stdout:
Server running: OK
=== Tunnel output ===
your url is: https://rich-nights-win.loca.lt

New download link: https://rich-nights-win.loca.lt/app-release.apk

=== Testing tunnel ===

server: SimpleHTTP/0.6 Python/3.13.14
date: Mon, 13 Jul 2026 11:30:09 GMT
content-type: application/vnd.android.package-archive
content-length: 82735802
last-modified: Mon, 13 Jul 2026 11:22:17 GMT
x-robots-tag: noindex, nofollow, noarchive, nosnippet, nositelinksearchbox, noimageindex
x-localtunnel-agent-ips: ["35.252.150.63"]
keep-alive: timeout=5

responseMessageContents.title: Create a new download link for the Android mobile app.
responseMessageContents.description: A new link was created to download the latest version of the application to fix errors that occurred when trying to open the app on Android phones.
